content:
- Full verification gate passed: make check, make test, make test-long (including Docker smoke), and make lint all succeeded.
- Updated the task file to completed/pass true with execution notes, then ran .ralph/task_switch.sh to mark the task finished in Ralph.
- Next is the final repo handoff: stage all changes including .ralph updates, commit with required task-finished message including test evidence/challenges, and push.

- Read previous Choose-Task progress, current active task summary, and done-task summary.
- Verified the active backlog currently contains four failing non-bug tasks and no active failing bug tasks, so bug-first did not force selection into the bugs story.
- Checked the concrete task files for blocker tags and priority tags. None of the four active tasks had <blocked_by>, and the visible priorities were low except the typed-endpoints task which had no explicit priority tag.
- Compared the two active switchover-story tasks. Chose `.ralph/tasks/story-switchover-operator-model/task-redesign-switchover-request-semantics-for-operators.md` because it defines the operator-semantic decision that the narrower legacy-request-field cleanup plus optional `switchover_to` implementation task depends on conceptually, even though no formal blocker tag exists yet.
- Wrote the selected task path into `.ralph/current_task.txt` and should quit immediately now.

- Read Ralph progress and current task path, then opened the active task file for redesigning switchover request semantics.
- Confirmed the task is in initial planning state: no TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE marker yet, so this turn should only write the implementation plan and stop.
- Audited the current code paths enough to make the plan concrete: API/CLI/DCS all require a legacy requester field, but HA only checks request presence and chooses successor automatically from observed cluster state; docs and tests currently expose the misleading field widely.
- Next I should patch the task file with a detailed execution plan that commits to generic switchover semantics, add TO BE VERIFIED at the end, append this note, and quit immediately.

- Read the active switchover semantics task and confirmed it was still in the initial planning phase.
- Audited the concrete source touchpoints across API, CLI, DCS, HA decisions, tests, and docs, then wrote a detailed execution plan directly into the task file.
- The plan commits to generic switchover semantics with no caller-supplied identity, renames operator-visible status away from the old requester-shaped field, and explicitly defers targeted switchover because the current HA engine only supports automatic successor selection.
- Appended the required `TO BE VERIFIED` marker and stopped without executing code changes, because the workflow requires a separate deep skeptical review pass next.

- Read the active switchover semantics task in TO BE VERIFIED state and did a skeptical review against concrete code paths.
- Confirmed the review must change the plan materially: the public POST endpoint is still `/switchover`, the replacement HA state field should be fixed explicitly to `switchover_pending: bool` instead of leaving it tentative, and stale docs under docs/draft/docs and docs/tmp also reference the old names.
- Noted that the task plan currently mentions an `update-docs` skill, but that skill is not available in this session, so the verified plan needs to call out manual docs updates/regeneration instead of relying on a missing skill.
- Next step is to patch the task file plan with those corrections, replace `TO BE VERIFIED` with `NOW EXECUTE`, append this note, and stop without implementing code.

- Patched the task file during the verification pass and replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE.
- The verified plan now locks in the exact public response field `switchover_pending: bool`, explicitly keeps POST on `/switchover`, and adds manual docs refresh/prune requirements for docs/draft/docs and docs/tmp because the `update-docs` skill is unavailable in this session.
- Stopping here per workflow: this turn only verified and corrected the plan, and the next turn should execute the plan without more exploration.

- Read the active task file and confirmed it is already in NOW EXECUTE state from the previous passes.
- Switching from planning/review to implementation now; per workflow I will avoid broad exploration and only read the concrete files named in the task plan to patch API, CLI, HA, tests, and docs.
- Need to keep the public POST endpoint on /switchover, replace the legacy requester-shaped semantics with `switchover_pending`, run all required gates, then update the task file completion state.

- Replaced the core switchover model in Rust with an empty DCS marker request and a bool HA/API surface named switchover_pending.
- Cleared most stale legacy-field references across API, CLI, HA, DCS, and tests; the remaining fallout is limited to a couple of multi-node helper call sites plus docs.
- A first focused cargo test command was malformed because cargo accepts one filter at a time; next I am fixing the last call sites and rerunning with package-wide or single-filter test invocations.

- Rust library tests now pass after the switchover model rewrite; the broad switchover filter was misleading because it pulled in long-running e2e coverage.
- Updated the main operator docs plus key tracked draft mirrors to remove the stale requester-style field and document switchover_pending plus automatic successor selection.
- Next I am formatting and then running the required full gates in order: make check, make test, make test-long, make lint.

- Full verification gate passed after the switchover rewrite: make check, make test, make test-long (including Docker smoke), and make lint all succeeded.
- Marked the active task file done with passes=true and execution notes; next is task switch, stage all changes including .ralph evidence/task state, commit with gate evidence in the message, and push.
- No AGENTS.md update looks necessary because this task did not reveal a reusable repo-specific surprise beyond what is already documented.
